Publications
text:
Journal PLOS ONE · May 2024


Automatic quantification of tumor-stroma ratio as a prognostic biomarker for pancreatic cancer


Vendittelli P, Bokhorst J-M, Smeets EMM, Kryklyva V, Brosens LAA, Verbeke C, Litjens G.


Multi-class U-Net for TSR segmentation across 368 patients / 4 datasets (25 NL labs). Dice 0.75 (epithelium) and 0.86 (tumor bulk) on external TCGA-PAAD validation. TSR AUC 0.61 for 6-month survival prediction on an independent cohort.

Automatic tumour segmentation in H&E-stained whole-slide images of the pancreas


Vendittelli P, Smeets EMM, Litjens G.


Multi-task U-Net with auxiliary classification head for coarse pancreatic tumour area segmentation; classification head reduced false positives.

title: Pierpaolo Vendittelli
text:
Pierpaolo Vendittelli


Postdoc Researcher · Medical AI & Computational Pathology


📍 Nijmegen, The Netherlands  ·  Open to Italy · Remote EU · US-remote

I am Pierpaolo Vendittelli, a postdoctoral researcher developing deep-learning systems for digital pathology, spanning whole-slide image segmentation, multi-center clinical AI pipelines, prognostic modeling, and downstream applications of foundation models. My current work focuses on Wilms tumor grading at the Prinses Maxima Center for Pediatric Oncology. During my PhD at Radboud University Medical Center, I worked on pancreatic cancer prognosis, tumor segmentation, and tumor microenvironment quantification. I also design LLM-based automation tools to streamline research workflows and professional social media management. This portfolio brings together my publications, projects, CV, and ongoing learning log.
Open to Applied Scientist, ML Engineer, and R&D roles across medical AI and applied ML. PhD defense: June 1, 2026.

  View CV

title: Curriculum Vitae
text:
Experience


Dec 2024 – Present


Postdoc Researcher — Prinses Maxima Centre for Pediatric Oncology, Utrecht, NL

Developing deep-learning and MIL pipelines for WSI analysis of Wilms tumor, targeting anaplasia grading and clinical outcome prediction.
Coordinating multi-center dataset integration across collaborating hospitals.
Translating model outputs into interpretable biomarkers in collaboration with pathologists and pediatric oncologists.
Supervising students; contributing to peer-reviewed publications.





Mar 2021 – Jun 2026 (exp.)


PhD Candidate — Artificial Intelligence — Radboud University Medical Center, Nijmegen, NL

Developed deep-learning pipelines for pancreatic cancer WSI analysis within PANCAIM (EU Horizon 2020): multi-class U-Net for TSR quantification (368 patients / 4 datasets / 25 NL labs; Dice 0.75–0.86; TSR AUC 0.61 on TCGA-PAAD). Published in PLOS ONE (2024) and SPIE MI (2022).
Extended to multi-tissue segmentation for combining AI-based biomarkers (TSR, mitosis density, stromal cell density, TTR) in multivariate PDAC survival analysis. Published at COMPAYL (MICCAI 2025 workshop).
Co-authored multi-center clinical studies: European Radiology (2025) on multimodal prognostication; The Lancet Oncology (2025) PANORAMA study; Medical Image Analysis (2025) scoping review on multimodal AI.
Teaching Assistant for Image Analysis and Medical Imaging Analysis (4 course editions, Radboud University); co-supervised Master’s theses.





Oct 2020 – Feb 2021


Machine Learning Engineer — Everis Italy (NTT DATA), Rome, IT

Built AI chatbots and conversational systems for clients in banking and automotive.
Annotated industrial data for object recognition systems and developed live translation pipelines for video content.





Feb 2020 – Sep 2020


Research Intern — ViCOROB – NeuroImage Computing Group, University of Girona, ES

Benchmarked MRI synthesis architectures (ResU-Net, cGAN, CycleGAN) on WMH and BraTS 2018 datasets.
ResU-Net showed superior SSIM and lower MSE vs. GAN variants under data-scarce conditions; informed Master’s thesis (grade 8.6/10).

title: Curriculum Vitae
text:
Selected Projects

Social Agent (2026–present): Human-in-the-loop agent for personal technical communication. Captures private Telegram notes and GitHub milestones, ranks ideas, drafts LLM-assisted posts, routes approvals via Telegram, and schedules publishing with a separate private state store and tests.
AI Job-Search Agent (2025–present): End-to-end pipeline for ingesting job-alert emails, parsing structured vacancies, deduplicating in SQLite, ranking with an LLM, and sending Telegram digests on a daily schedule.
Weekly AI Paper-Triage Agent (2024–present): Multi-agent workflow that scans arXiv and PubMed, summarizes candidate papers, scores relevance, and emails a weekly digest for literature triage.

title: Projects
text:
Personal Project · 2026


AI Social Agent


AI-assisted workflow for managing a professional X presence with human-in-the-loop approval. It monitors GitHub repositories for milestone-worthy updates, processes private notes and photos via a Telegram inbox, and generates ranked post drafts for review. The system learns from user feedback (approvals/rejections/edits) to refine its persona over time, while maintaining a strict privacy boundary through a separate private state repository.
Tech: Python · OpenAI API · X API · Telegram Bot API · GitHub Actions
GitHub




Personal Project · 2024


Weekly AI Paper-Triage Agent


Automated literature monitoring pipeline that fetches weekly arXiv and PubMed papers, performs LLM-based summarisation via OpenAI API (gpt-4o-mini), and scores each paper 0–10 based on a personalised interest profile. Results are compiled into an email digest, with deduplication and structured outputs handled in Python. Runs on GitHub Actions once per week with no manual intervention, and has been in active personal use since 2024.
Tech: Python · OpenAI API · Gmail API · SQLite · GitHub Actions
GitHub




Personal Project · 2026


AI Job Search Agent


Automated job-hunting pipeline that fetches LinkedIn, Indeed, and Glassdoor alert emails via Gmail API, parses structured job listings, deduplicates against a SQLite store, and ranks each role 0–1 with Haiku4.5 against a personal résumé and preferences profile. Top matches are pushed as a ranked Telegram digest. Runs on GitHub Actions three times per day with no manual intervention.
Tech: Python · OpenAI API · Gmail API · SQLite · GitHub Actions · Telegram Bot API
GitHub




PhD Dissertation · Radboud University Medical Center · 2021 – 2024


Learning from Histopathology Images: AI-Driven Biomarkers for Pancreatic Ductal Adenocarcinoma


PhD thesis focusing on the development of artificial intelligence methods for digital pathology in pancreatic ductal adenocarcinoma (PDAC). It addresses major challenges in prognostic assessment through automated tumor segmentation, biomarker quantification, and survival prediction from whole-slide histopathology images. A central contribution is the development of robust pipelines for tumor-stroma ratio analysis and other interpretable morphological biomarkers associated with patient outcome. The work also evaluates multiple deep learning feature extraction strategies for their ability to predict overall survival across different datasets. The findings demonstrate the potential of computational pathology to improve the speed, consistency, and clinical relevance of histopathological assessment in pancreatic cancer. Overall, the thesis contributes scalable and interpretable AI-based tools that support prognostic modeling and future precision oncology research.
GitHub Download PDF




Open Source Framework


Pathology Segmentation Pipeline


A Docker-based pipeline for training and deploying deep learning segmentation models on Whole Slide Images (WSI). Built around an asynchronous tile inference engine, it supports tissue/background segmentation, multi-tissue classification, epithelium segmentation, and tumour-stroma ratio (TSR) computation for Pancreatic Cancer WSIs. The pipeline is also available as a pre-built image on DockerHub at pierpaolov93/pathology-pipeline:latest.
Tech: PyTorch · Docker · HuggingFace · Python
GitHub DockerHub




Postdoc Research · Prinses Maxima Centre · 2025 - Present


Wilms Tumor Anaplasia Classification


Weakly supervised deep learning pipeline for anaplasia classification in Wilms tumor WSIs, using foundation model features (Virchow) and multiple instance learning (AMIL) alongside slide-level encodings (PRISM). Trained on a multi-center dataset (24 patients, 188 WSIs) with patient-wise cross-validation to prevent leakage, achieving AUC > 0.85 across configurations. Integrates feature extraction, MIL aggregation, and robust evaluation for clinical outcome prediction, with planned external validation to assess generalisability.
Tech: PyTorch · MIL · Docker · multi-center data pipelines
GitHub




PhD Research · Radboud University Medical Center · 2021 – 2024


Automatic Quantification of Tumor-Stroma Ratio as a prognostic biomarker for pancreatic cancer.


Automated WSI analysis pipeline for pancreatic cancer, combining deep learning–based segmentation of tumor epithelium, tumor bulk, and stroma with downstream biomarker extraction. Built a multi-step system leveraging IHC-guided supervision and U-Net models to enable fully automatic tumor–stroma ratio (TSR) quantification across multi-center datasets. Achieved robust external segmentation performance (Dice up to 0.86) and demonstrated TSR as a reproducible prognostic feature (AUC ~0.61 for 6-month survival).
Tech: PyTorch · MONAI · Docker · SLURM · Python
GitHub




PhD Research · Radboud University Medical Center · 2021 – 2024


Automatic Tumor segmentation in PDAC WSIs


Automatic tumor segmentation pipeline for pancreatic cancer WSIs, using multi-task convolutional neural networks to jointly model detection and segmentation. Evaluated across multiple resolutions with patch-based training, showing that lower-resolution contextual features improve performance. The multi-task architecture reduced false positives and achieved Dice up to ~0.93 on cross-validation, outperforming single-task baselines. Designed as a foundational component for downstream biomarker extraction and clinical AI pipelines.
Tech: Python · PyTorch · Docker · SLURM
GitHub




Master’s Thesis · University of Girona · 2020


MRI Synthesis: ResU-Net vs. GAN Benchmarking


Benchmarked ResU-Net, cGAN, and CycleGAN for T1↔︎FLAIR MRI synthesis on WMH and BraTS 2018 datasets. ResU-Net achieved superior SSIM and lower MSE under data-scarce conditions. Grade: 8.6/10.
Tech: Keras · TensorFlow · Python · medical image registration
GitHub
